title: [OM] Need IntegerType? (or IR/LowerClasses bug)
body:
Input:
```
FIRRTL version 3.2.0
circuit ListCreate:
  module ListCreate:
    input propIn : Integer
    output propOut : List<Integer>

    propassign propOut, List<Integer>(propIn, Integer(123))
```

Error, with `firtool --mlir-print-ir-after-failure`:
```
list-create.fir:7:25: error: 'om.list_create' op requires all operands to have the same type
    propassign propOut, List<Integer>(propIn, Integer(123))
                        ^
list-create.fir:7:25: note: see current operation: %1 = "om.list_create"(%arg0, %0) : (i64, si12) -> !om.list<i64>
// -----// IR Dump After LowerClasses Failed (firrtl-lower-classes) //----- //
"firrtl.circuit"() ({
  "firrtl.module"() ({
  ^bb0:
  }) {annotations = [], convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>, portAnnotations = [], portDirections = 0 : i0, portLocations = [], portNames = [], portSyms = [], portTypes = [], sym_name = "ListCreate"} : () -> ()
  "om.class"() ({
  ^bb0(%arg0: i64):
    %0 = "om.constant"() {value = 123 : si12} : () -> si12
    %1 = "om.list_create"(%arg0, %0) : (i64, si12) -> !om.list<i64>
    "om.class.field"(%1) {sym_name = "propOut"} : (!om.list<i64>) -> ()
  }) {formalParamNames = ["propIn"], sym_name = "ListCreate_Class"} : () -> ()
}) {annotations = [], name = "ListCreate"} : () -> ()
```

Since these are APInt's/BigInt's, probably need a type to capture any width integer so, e.g., `List<Integer>` works at the OM level.
